description: Differential pressure drop in Cleanroom ISO Class 7 (CR-04) below the validated limit of 15 Pa. Pressure dropped to 8 Pa for 25 minutes during active aseptic filling of Batch #INJ-2025-040.
During QA review of the Batch Manufacturing Record (BMR) for Batch #TBL-2025-450, a missing operator signature was identified at the blending step.
Incorrect Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) dispensed for Batch #CAP-2025-112. Operator dispensed 'API-X' instead of 'API-Y' due to highly similar container labeling and adjacent storage.
Routine daily calibration verification of pH meter EQ-PHM-12 failed using pH 4.01 buffer. Meter reading was 4.15 (Limit: ± 0.05).
Planned deviation to use Blender EQ-BLND-03 instead of the primary Blender EQ-BLND-01 for Batch #TBL-2025-455 due to scheduled preventive maintenance on the primary unit.
Incorrect expiry date overprinted on cartons for Batch #OINT-2025-05. Printed expiry was '10/2026' instead of the approved '10/2027'.
Microbial count for Purified Water at user point POU-05 was 150 cfu/mL, exceeding the action limit of 100 cfu/mL.
Jacket temperature of formulation vessel EQ-VES-10 overshot the set point by 3°C for 10 minutes during the melting phase of Batch #CRM-2025-88.
Dissolution test failure at 3-month accelerated stability time point for Batch #TBL-2025-101. Only 70% released at 45 minutes (Limit: NLT 80%).
Leak test failure for blister strips of Batch #CAP-2025-200. Dye ingress observed in 4 out of 10 sampled blister pockets during in-process checks.

Autoclave ID: EQ-AUTO-02 failed to reach the required sterilization temperature of 121.1°C for the mandatory hold time of 15 minutes. The temperature fluctuated between 118°C and 119.5°C during Cycle #22.
Stability chamber EQ-STB-05 (Set point: 25°C/60% RH) experienced a temperature excursion reaching 29°C for 4 hours due to a temporary power outage.
Failure of the Integrated Particle Counter (IPC) on the aseptic filling line EQ-FILL-04. Signal loss occurred during Batch #VAX-2025-99.
Planned deviation for a 14-day extension of the hold time for Granulated Bulk Batch #TBL-2025-998 due to machine breakdown.
Raw Material 'Excipient-Z' (Lot #ABC-123) found in the warehouse with water-damaged exterior packaging. Possible moisture exposure.
During inspection of the QC Lab, it was observed that usage log for HPLC instrument EQ-HPLC-08 had an overwriting without initial and date, violating GDP.
Total Organic Carbon (TOC) levels in the Purified Water system (Loop B) peaked at 800 ppb, exceeding the action limit of 500 ppb for 45 minutes.
A 'Missing Tablet' in a blister pocket was discovered by a manual packer downstream of the Automatic Vision Inspection System. The system failed to reject the defective blister card.
The font size used for the 'Batch Number' on the primary packaging labels (Batch #OINT-2025-88) was slightly smaller (8pt) than the approved version (10pt).
Residue from the previous product (Batch #SUGAR-001) was found in the bottom discharge valve of formulation vessel after 'Cleaned' status had been signed off.
Sterility test failure for Batch #ABC-123. Microbial growth (Staphylococcus aureus) observed in 2 out of 20 canisters during 14-day incubation.
rootCauses: Aseptic technique breach during media transfer

description: Temperature excursion in the walk-in stability chamber (QC-STB-01). Temp peaked at 32°C (Limit: 25±2°C) for 6 hours due to a faulty thermostat.
rootCauses: Mechanical failure of the thermostat relay

description: Particulate matter (visible glass fragment) found in one vial of Batch #VAX-777 during manual inspection after filling.
rootCauses: Vial damage on starwheel during transport segment

description: ISO Class 5 air velocity drop below 0.36 m/s in the aseptic filling zone for 15 minutes during shift change.
rootCauses: BMS system recalibration error after power cycle

description: Aluminum foil roll for 10mg tablets swapped with 20mg foil during secondary packaging setup for Batch #TBL-009.
rootCauses: Failure of second-person verification during line clearance

description: Yield loss of 15% observed for Granulation Batch #GRN-010. The loss exceeded the validated limit of 5% during the final discharge process.
rootCauses:
1. Misalignment of discharge chute (Equipment)
2. Failure to monitor waste collection bin (Human Error)

description: Deviating from the approved 'PV-TAB-05' Process Validation protocol. The sampling frequency for the compression step was reduced from every 15 minutes to every 30 minutes.
rootCauses: Lack of clarity in the simplified protocol instructions provided to the operator.

description: Finished product pallets of Batch #CRM-889 were temporarily stored in an unmapped warehouse corridor for 12 hours. Ambient temperature reached 31°C.
rootCauses: Lack of available space in the temperature-controlled zone due to overstocking.

description: Out-of-Specification (OOS) result observed for Assay (94.2%) of Aspirin Tablets Batch #ASP-101 at the 6-month stability time point.
rootCauses: Oversight in the accelerated stability chamber temperature regulation logic (Equipment).

description: Temperature in the Microbiology Incubator (EQ-INC-05) dropped to 22°C for 8 hours while incubating environmental monitoring plates (Required: 30-35°C).
rootCauses: Faulty compressor heating element caused by recent power surge.

description: Temperature in the Microbiology Incubator (EQ-INC-05) dropped to 22°C for 8 hours while incubating environmental monitoring plates (Required: 30-35°C).
rootCauses: Faulty compressor heating element caused by recent power surge.

description: Compressed air pressure in the tablet coating pan (EQ-CTP-03) dropped from 6 bar to 3.5 bar mid-cycle during film coating of Batch #TBL-811, causing incomplete coating.
rootCauses: Blocked inline air filter causing downstream pressure loss.

description: Sticky patches observed on Batch #ORL-301 oral tablets after 3 days in the bulk container. Tablets were clumping together during in-process inspection.
rootCauses: Excess residual moisture in granules due to insufficient drying time in the fluidized bed dryer.

description: Water leakage observed from the ceiling in Area A-101.
rootCauses: Damaged water pipe due to corrosion.

description: Omission of mandatory technician signatures in the manufacturing logbooks during the tablet compression stage.
rootCauses: Personnel exhaustion during transition between night and morning shifts; inadequate supervisory review.

description: Operator failed to sign the batch manufacturing record (BMR) for the granulation step in real-time.
rootCauses: High workload during shift change and lack of oversight by the supervisor.
